01_initial_request.input_data: Existing literature on fluorescent probes and biological samples (e.g., liver microsomes, living organisms)
01_initial_request.user_intent: To summarize recent advances in fluorescent probes for CYP450s to enhance the diversity, practicality, and clinical feasibility of CYP450 research and drug safety
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Selective detection and monitoring with high temporal and spatial resolution imaging in situ

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] CYP450 enzymes catalyze oxidation reactions like hydroxylation and dealkylation, which are crucial for drug metabolism. [Gap] Traditional probes lack isoform specificity and deep tissue imaging capabilities. [Decision] To achieve selective detection, chose to use Virtual Screening Strategy to perform two-dimensional design on putative two-photon fluorescent substrates, expecting to obtain optimized fluorophore structures with improved efficiency and cost.

parameters.design_dimension_1: efficient program to virtually screen putative two-photon fluorescent substrates
parameters.design_dimension_2: adjust the reaction rate and isoform selectivity by modifying non-reactive sites
parameters.fluorophore_type: N-butyl and N-phenyl derivatives of naphthalimide (NN)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Probe 16 (NEN) was developed to monitor CYP3A4 activity with high selectivity.

references.citation: Ning, J., et al. (2019). Target Enzyme-Activated Two-Photon Fluorescent Probes: A Case Study of CYP3A4 Using a Two-Dimensional Design Strategy. Angew. Chem. Int. Ed.
references.relevance: Describes the two-dimensional design strategy for CYP3A4 probes.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] The active cavity structure of CYP450 isoforms varies, affecting substrate binding. [Gap] Theoretical binding affinity needs verification to ensure specificity before synthesis. [Decision] To achieve isoform specificity, chose to use Molecular Docking to perform reverse protein-ligand docking on fluorescent substrate candidates, expecting to obtain theoretical distance from catalytic site to reaction site.

parameters.selection_criteria: theoretical distance from the catalytic site to the reaction site
parameters.inactive_threshold: considered as not catalytically active when the theoretical distance of the catalytic reaction is exceeded

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Potential CYP450 isozyme-specific probes were identified, such as Probe 13 (MXMB) for CYP2J2.

references.citation: Feng, L., et al. (2022). A practical strategy to develop isoform-selective near-infrared fluorescent probes for human cytochrome P450 enzymes. Acta Pharm. Sin. B.
references.relevance: Describes the double-filtering strategy including reverse docking.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Docking results provide theoretical binding modes but require dynamic stability assessment. [Gap] Static docking does not account for conformational flexibility and energy stability. [Decision] To achieve higher specificity, chose to use Molecular Dynamics Simulation to perform free energy calculations on the probe-enzyme complex, expecting to obtain match validation between probe and CYP450 characteristic isoforms.

parameters.target: match between the probe and CYP450 characteristic isoforms

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Probe 13 (MXMB) was confirmed for selective activation process of CYP2J2 in various tumor models.

references.citation: Feng, L., et al. (2022). A practical strategy to develop isoform-selective near-infrared fluorescent probes for human cytochrome P450 enzymes. Acta Pharm. Sin. B.
references.relevance: Mentions molecular dynamics simulations and free energy calculations as part of the screening process.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Virtual candidates need to be physically realized for biological testing. [Gap] Computational models cannot confirm actual fluorescence response in biological systems. [Decision] To achieve functional probes, chose to use Chemical Synthesis to perform local modification at the probe recognition site with linker arms and alkyl groups, expecting to obtain synthesized fluorescent probes like BnXPI or NCMN.

parameters.modification_strategy: proximal local modification strategy close to the catalytic site

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Probes such as Probe 1 (NCMN), Probe 11 (BnXPI), and Probe 8 (BCy-CYP) were successfully synthesized.

references.citation: Dai, Z.R., et al. (2015). A Highly Selective Ratiometric Two-Photon Fluorescent Probe for Human Cytochrome P450 1A. J. Am. Chem. Soc.
Ning, J., et al. (2019). Molecular Design Strategy to Construct the Near-Infrared Fluorescent Probe for Selectively Sensing Human Cytochrome P450 2J2. J. Am. Chem. Soc.
references.relevance: Describes synthesis of BnXPI probe with linker arm.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Synthesized probes must be tested for sensitivity and selectivity against CYP isoforms. [Gap] In silico predictions need experimental validation of enzymatic activity. [Decision] To achieve quantitative detection, chose to use Fluorescence Spectroscopy to perform in vitro enzyme assays on recombinant CYP450s, expecting to obtain Limit of Detection (LOD) and selectivity profiles.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Probe 4 (iPrBN) showed LOD=0.036 nM for CYP1A1; Probe 5 (DPCl) showed LOD=0.026 nM; Probe 9 (HBMN) showed 25x higher affinity for CYP1A2.

references.citation: Ning, J., et al. (2018). A highly sensitive and selective two-photon fluorescent probe for real-time sensing of cytochrome P450 1A1 in living systems. Mater. Chem. Front.
references.relevance: Reports LOD and selectivity for iPrBN.

references.citation: Xue, T.Z., et al. (2019). Ultrasensitive near-infrared fluorescent probe with large stokes shift for real-time tracing of CYP1A1 in living cells and zebrafish model. Sens. Actuators B.
Shangguan, L., et al. (2019). Highly sensitive fluorescent bioassay of 2,3,7,8-tetrachloro-dibenzo-p-dioxin based on abnormal expression of cytochrome P450 1A2 in human cells. Anal. Chim. Acta.
references.relevance: Reports affinity difference between CYP1A2 and CYP1A1 for HBMN.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] In vitro results need validation in complex cellular environments. [Gap] Enzyme activity in cells may differ due to localization and interference. [Decision] To achieve spatial resolution imaging, chose to use Confocal Microscopy to perform two-photon fluorescence imaging in living cells (e.g., HepG2), expecting to obtain subcellular localization and activity maps.

parameters.cell_lines: HepG2 cells, HCT-15 colon cancer cells

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Probe 1 (NCMN) detected CYP1A in HepG2 cells and rat liver slices at depth of ~100 um; Probe 11 (BnXPI) visualized CYP2J2 in cells.

references.citation: Dai, Z.R., et al. (2015). A Highly Selective Ratiometric Two-Photon Fluorescent Probe for Human Cytochrome P450 1A. J. Am. Chem. Soc.
references.relevance: Describes two-photon confocal imaging in HepG2 cells and liver slices.

references.citation: Ning, J., et al. (2019). Molecular Design Strategy to Construct the Near-Infrared Fluorescent Probe for Selectively Sensing Human Cytochrome P450 2J2. J. Am. Chem. Soc.
references.relevance: Describes imaging of CYP2J2 in cells.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Cellular imaging does not reflect whole-organism pharmacokinetics or tumor microenvironment. [Gap] Clinical feasibility requires in vivo validation. [Decision] To achieve clinical feasibility, chose to use In Vivo Imaging System to perform NIR fluorescence imaging in tumor-bearing mice, expecting to obtain tumor uptake and distribution data.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Probe 10 (6b) confirmed rapid accumulation in HCT-15 tumors; Probe 11 visualized CYP2J2 in subcutaneous tumors; Probe 17 tracked CYP3A5 in tumor-bearing mice.

references.citation: Meng, Q.Q., et al. (2018). Design, Synthesis, and Biological Evaluation of Cytochrome P450 1B1 Targeted Molecular Imaging Probes for Colorectal Tumor Detection. J. Med. Chem.
references.relevance: Describes in vivo NIR imaging in HCT-15 tumor-bearing mice.

references.citation: Ji, H.F., et al. (2021). A near-infrared ratiometric fluorescent probe for selectively tracking human cytochrome P450 3A5 in living cells and tumor-bearing mice. Sens. Actuators B.
references.relevance: Describes in vivo imaging of CYP3A5.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Fluorescence signals indicate activity but not metabolic products. [Gap] Mechanism of metabolism and Drug-Drug Interactions (DDI) require metabolite identification. [Decision] To achieve mechanism validation, chose to use LC-MS to perform metabolite identification and inhibitor studies, expecting to obtain confirmation of catalytic reaction and MBI effects.

parameters.comparison: LC-MS and HPLC vs optical signal changes

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Probe 18 (BN-1) successfully traced and evaluated MBI behaviors of common medicines; Probe 9 response depended on LC-MS/HPLC initially.

references.citation: Ning, J., et al. (2022). Rational Design of a Two-Photon Fluorescent Probe for Human Cytochrome P450 3A and the Visualization of Mechanism-Based Inactivation. Angew. Chem. Int. Ed.
Wu, J.J., et al. (2017). A Naturally Occurring Isoform-Specific Probe for Highly Selective and Sensitive Detection of Human Cytochrome P450 3A5. J. Med. Chem.
references.relevance: Notes characterization depended on LC-MS and HPLC.

limit_of_detection_CYP1A1.interpretation: High sensitivity achieved for CYP1A1 detection using Probe 4 (iPrBN).

limit_of_detection_CYP1A1_NIR.interpretation: Ultrasensitive detection achieved using NIR Probe 5 (DPCl).

selectivity_fold_CYP1A2.interpretation: Probe 9 (HBMN) showed 25 times higher affinity for CYP1A2 than CYP1A1.

imaging_depth.interpretation: Two-photon imaging achieved depth of ~100 um in fresh rat liver slice.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective to summarize recent advances in fluorescent probes for CYP450s was achieved. The extracted workflow demonstrates that selective detection and monitoring with high temporal and spatial resolution imaging in situ is feasible using the described probes (e.g., NCMN, iPrBN, BnXPI, BN-1), enhancing diversity, practicality, and clinical feasibility.
